message:
fix: io/split !Send docs, tcp keepalive simplification, gf2 highest_set_bit mask, token slab bounds, and clippy/style cleanups

Nine targeted fixes across the codebase:

1. **io/split docs (split.rs)**: Clarified that split halves use
   RefCell and are !Send, so they cannot be spawned into separate
   tasks.  Directed users to owned splits (e.g. TcpStream::into_split)
   for cross-task concurrent use.

2. **TCP keepalive (tcp/stream.rs)**: set_keepalive() simplified from
   a match expression to keepalive.map_or(Disabled, Enabled).

3. **GF2 highest_set_bit (trace/gf2.rs)**: highest_set_bit() now
   masks out bits past self.len in the last word before scanning,
   preventing false positives when the word contains set bits beyond
   the logical vector length.

4. **Token slab bounds (reactor/token.rs)**: insert() now asserts
   entries.len() < u32::MAX on 64-bit targets, preventing the slab
   from growing past the FREE_LIST_END sentinel value.

5. **Spork arm merge (spork.rs)**: Two identical Start(SpawnFailed)
   and Spawn arms merged into a single or-pattern.

6. **Progress truncation (cli/progress.rs)**: Replaced the
   .min(u128::from(u64::MAX)) pattern with an explicit if/else
   for clarity, since the .min() pattern is getting unwieldy.

7. **Task inspector time (task_inspector.rs)**: Test updated to use
   65s instead of 5s to exercise the stuck-task threshold (60s).

8. **Obligation clippy (symbolic_obligation.rs)**: Added
   allow(struct_field_names) on RegistryMirror and
   allow(significant_drop_tightening) on the two removal helpers.
   Unregister method simplified with is_some_and().

9. **Router slice ref (transport/router.rs)**: select_n_weighted_round_robin
   now takes &[&Arc<Endpoint>] instead of owned Vec, avoiding a
   needless move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
